lighting.time_of_day: Golden hour transitioning to blue hour — warm amber sun or deep twilight indigo, never midday
lighting.key_light: Backlit or 45° side light — warm amber #d4a574 at sunset, cool cyan-blue #2a5a7a at twilight, creating strong rim-light on subjects
lighting.fill_light: Soft atmospheric ambient — low-intensity fill with visible haze, dust, or volumetric fog reducing contrast by 30-40%
lighting.shadow_character: Soft to medium gradient shadows — luminous in golden hour (warm bronze undertones), obsidian in blue hour (deep navy-black)

palette.dominant_warm: #d4a574 amber gold transitioning to #d97234 burnt terracotta
palette.dominant_cool: #1a4d6f deep teal-blue transitioning to #1a2a4a indigo night
palette.ratio: 45/45/10 warm/cool/accent — strong chromatic split between sky and land, or foreground and background
palette.saturation: Medium-high — desert and nature scenes at 65-80% saturation, studio/festival scenes at 70-90%

subject_framing.subject_width_pct: 30-50% — subjects occupy less than half the frame, allowing landscape/atmosphere to breathe
subject_framing.subject_position: Center or lower third — rule-of-thirds horizontal placement, subjects often grounded at bottom 40% of frame
subject_framing.subject_gaze: Looking down or away — 24/30 subjects focused on instrument or horizon, not camera; eyes closed or averted creates introspective mood
subject_framing.crop_style: Wide establishing or medium waist-up — prefer environmental context over tight portraits; 18mm-35mm equivalent focal length feel

scene_depth.foreground: Musician with instrument anchoring composition — handpan, keyboard, or duo positioned in lower 40% of frame
scene_depth.midground: Performance space or crowd — festival crowd silhouettes, desert floor with tribal torches, or studio floor with visible texture
scene_depth.background: Expansive natural or architectural drama — mountain ranges, desert horizons, pyramids, abandoned interiors, or festival stage with sky
scene_depth.atmospheric_effect: Haze, dust, or volumetric fog in 21/30 thumbnails — creates depth separation and diffuses background by 20-40%

shock_element.description: The handpan instrument itself (metallic UFO-like dome with visible tone fields) or massive festival crowd scale — the handpan's alien-organic hybrid form is instantly recognizable and genre-specific; festival shots use 10,000+ crowd density to convey cultural movement scale
shock_element.frequency: Handpan visible in 9/30 thumbnails; massive crowds (5,000+ people) in 4/30; surreal artistic compositions (floating objects, extreme color treatments, surrealist collage) in 7/30

anti_patterns: No midday harsh sun or flat noon lighting — all images use transitional light (sunrise, sunset, twilight, or controlled studio gels)
No tight headshot crops or portrait-only framing — always include environmental context, instrument, or performance space
No clean minimal backgrounds — every background has texture, atmosphere, or geographic specificity (desert, forest, ruins, festival)
No subjects staring directly at camera with posed smiles — introspective, meditative, or performance-focused gaze only
No cluttered multi-element collages or busy graphic overlays — when text is present, it's bold typographic lockup with ample negative space, not layered effects
prompt_preamble: Photographed with 24mm wide-angle lens at f/2.8 creating subtle environmental blur beyond 8 meters. Golden hour backlight from 30° behind subject casting warm amber #d4a574 rim-light, or deep blue hour twilight with indigo #1a2a4a sky gradient. Soft atmospheric haze reducing background contrast by 35%, visible dust particles or volumetric fog in midground. Subject occupies 35-45% of frame width, positioned in lower third, eyes averted downward toward instrument or gazing at distant horizon. Three-layer depth: foreground musician with handpan or ethnic instrument in sharp focus, midground performance space with visible texture (sand ripples, weathered stone, festival crowd), background expansive desert mountain range or twilight sky with 25% atmospheric diffusion. Color palette: 45% warm amber-terracotta #d4a574 to #d97234, 45% cool teal-indigo #1a4d6f to #1a2a4a, 10% magenta-rose #c44569 accent. Saturation 70-80%. Soft gradient shadows with luminous bronze undertones in golden hour or obsidian navy-black in blue hour. Environmental storytelling over portraiture — always include geographic or cultural context. Meditative, introspective mood. Never midday sun, never direct camera gaze, never clean studio background.
